content:

title: The Downward Road.
content:
Two Yankee maids of simple mien,
    And earnest, high endeavour,
    Come sailing to the land of France,
    To escape the winter weather.

title: Mountain--Laurel
content:

    The world grows brighter to my eyes,
    And summer comes with thee.

content:

title: Fairy Song.
content:
The moonlight fades from flower and tree,
    And the stars dim one by one;

title: To One Who Teaches Me
content:
"To one who teaches me
    The sweetness and the beauty
    Of doing faithfully
    And cheerfully my duty."

title: From The Short Story A Christmas Dream, And How It Came True by Louisa May Alcott
content:
From our happy home
Through the world we roam
One week in all the year,
Making winter spring
With the joy we bring
For Christmas-tide is here.

Now th

title: The Rose Family - Song II by Louisa May Alcott
content:
O lesson well and wisely taught
Stay with me to the last,
That all my life may better be
For the trial that is past.
O vanity, mislead no more!
Sleep,

title: The Rose Family - Song 1 by Louisa May Alcott
content:

Why blossom you so fair,
With your green and purple cup
Upturned to sun and air?

title: From The Short Story Shadow-Children by Louisa May Alcott
content:
Little shadows, little shadows
Dancing on the chamber wall,
While I sit beside the hearthstone
Where the red flames rise and fall.

title: Lily-Bell
content:

title: I Write About The Butterfly
content:
"I write about the butterfly,
        It is a pretty thing;
        And flies about like the birds,
        But it does not sing.

title: Give Me Freshening Breeze, My Boys
content:

title: Rock A Bye, Babies
content:

title: To J.M.B.
content:
'Oh, were I a heliotrope,
        I would play poet,
        And blow a breeze of fragrance
        To you; and none should know it.

title: Tell The Dear Old Body
content:
'Tell the dear old body
        This day I cannot run,
        For the pots are boiling over
        And the mutton isn't done.'"

title: Chingery Wangery Chan
content:

        His name was Chingery Wangery Chan."

content:

title: I Slept, And Dreamed That Life Was Beauty
I Wish I Had A Quiet Tomb
content:
"I wish I had a quiet tomb,
        Beside a little rill;
        Where birds, and bees, and butterflies,
        Would sing upon the hill.

content:

To seek, to find, and make his own

title: Oh, Have You E'Er Heard Of Kate Kearney
content:

title: Oh My Heart Is Sad And Weary
content:
'Oh my heart is sad and weary
        Everywhere I roam,
        Longing for the old plantation
        And for the old folks at home.'

title: He That Is Down Need Fear No Fall
content:
He that is down need fear no fall,
        He that is low no pride.
        He that is humble ever shall
        Have God to be his guide.

title: Sweet! Sweet!
content:

title: Morning Song Of The Bees
content:
"Awake! awake! for the earliest gleam
	Of golden sunlight shines
	On the rippling waves, that brightly flow
	Beneath the flowering vines.
	Awake!

title: Dear Grif
content:
"Dear Grif,
    Here is a whiff
    Of beautiful spring flowers;
    The big red rose
    Is for your nose,
    As toward the sky it towers.

title: Quee, Quee!
content:

title: To Papa
content:
In high Olympus' sacred shade
         A gift Minerva wrought
        For her beloved philosopher
         Immersed in deepest thought.

title: Work, Neighbor, Work!
content:

        Do not stop to play;
        Wander far and wide,
        Gather all you may.

content:

title: A Song From The Suds
content:
Queen of my tub, I merrily sing,

        And sturdily wash and rinse and wring,

content:
"Hello! hello!
        Come down below,--
        It's lovely and cool
        Out here in the pool;

content:

content:

        Harvests have been gathered
        Gayly one by one.

title: Sweet, Sweet Days Are Passing
content:
Sweet, sweet days are passing
        O'er my happy home.

title: The Jungfrau To Beth
content:
God bless you, dear Queen Bess!

        But health and peace and happiness

title: Flowers, dear flowers, farewell!
content:
"We are sending you, dear flowers,
    Forth alone to die,
    Where your gentle sisters may not weep
    O'er the cold graves where you lie;

title: He Prayeth Best Who Loveth Best
content:
"He prayeth best who loveth best
        All things, both great and small;
        For the dear God who loveth us,

title: My Kingdom
content:
A little kingdom I possess,
        Where thoughts and feelings dwell;
        And very hard I find the task
        Of governing it well.

content:
